advantages: Directly optimizes probability assigned to the true class, aligning training with probabilistic predictions.
Convex in logits for softmax/sigmoid models, yielding stable gradients and well-understood optimization.
Equivalent to maximum likelihood under Bernoulli/Categorical models, connecting theory and practice.
Decomposes cleanly into entropy H(P) plus KL penalty, linking to information theory and divergence measures.
Universal default for classification and language modeling with mature library support.
disadvantages: Heavily penalizes confident wrong predictions (−log q → ∞ as q(y) → 0), which can dominate batches with mislabeled or ambiguous examples.
Assumes a probabilistic label model; poorly suited as a sole objective when calibration or ranking matters more than class probabilities.
One-hot P ignores label uncertainty; without smoothing, models may become overconfident.
Class imbalance can skew average loss unless weighted or resampled — raw cross-entropy favors frequent classes.
Not a metric in the mathematical sense (unlike KL, it is not symmetric and depends on both P and Q).
